THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Marathon County learned it was denied FEMA public assistance after the April storms, though 24 residents have already applied for individual aid. Meanwhile, Kronenwetter and Weston boards moved several infrastructure and development items forward.

  • Marathon County was approved for FEMA individual assistance but denied public assistance due to insufficient documented damages; residents can apply at disasterassistance.gov.
  • Kronenwetter's Village Board approved a six-bay equipment garage bid at $814,815, added a second restroom to the original design, and approved ballot drop boxes for the August primary and November general elections.
  • Weston's Public Works Committee awarded the Concord Avenue and Bayberry Street reconstruction to HS Suns for approximately $2,248,819.90, and a Howland Avenue surveying contract to Point of Beginning for $12,500.
  • Weston's Plan Commission unanimously approved a conditional use permit for a 1,090 sq ft detached ADU at 4804 Shore Avenue and forwarded a recommendation to approve the 23-lot Bull Junior View final plat.
  • Kronenwetter's Plan Commission is set to consider rezoning Marathon County Highway Department property from B3, SF and AR to M2 General Industrial and a conditional use permit for a residential pond.

Coming up: Monday brings full Village of Weston and City of Wausau committee meetings, including Weston's Board of Trustees at 6:00 PM and Wausau's Public Health & Safety Committee at 5:15 PM. Tuesday, Wausau's Plan Commission meets at 5:00 PM, and Kronenwetter's Revised Administrative Policy Committee convenes at 5:30 PM.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
